Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Dumping Lego NXT firmware off of an existing brick (2025) 229 upvotes, 13 comments
  2. The Collective Ambition Behind Odysseus, a Game-Changing Sci-Fi Larp 62 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. CasNum 313 upvotes, 38 comments
  4. Some Words on WigglyPaint 71 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Emacs internals: Deconstructing Lisp_Object in C (Part 2) 101 upvotes, 16 comments
  6. Phi-4-reasoning-vision and the lessons of training a multimodal reasoning model 38 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. Sem – Semantic version control. Entity-level diffs on top of Git 55 upvotes, 8 comments
  8. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 90 upvotes, 18 comments
  9. LibreOffice: Request to the European Commission to adhere to its own guidances 59 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. FrameBook 33 upvotes, 5 comments
  11. Show HN: Curiosity – DIY 6" Newtonian Reflector Telescope 61 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Autoresearch: Agents researching on single-GPU nanochat training automatically 153 upvotes, 42 comments
  13. How to run Qwen 3.5 locally 315 upvotes, 99 comments
  14. The surprising whimsy of the Time Zone Database 164 upvotes, 54 comments
  15. SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI 92 upvotes, 27 comments
  16. Show HN: Eyot, A programming language where the GPU is just another thread 40 upvotes, 9 comments
  17. I ported Linux to the PS5 and turned it into a Steam Machine 139 upvotes, 49 comments
  18. Cloud VM benchmarks 2026 290 upvotes, 130 comments
  19. Notes on Writing WASM 108 upvotes, 43 comments
  20. From RGB to L*a*b* color space (2024) 69 upvotes, 25 comments
  21. Yoghurt delivery women combatting loneliness in Japan 349 upvotes, 185 comments
  22. Warn about PyPy being unmaintained 262 upvotes, 136 comments
  23. The Lobster Programming Language 73 upvotes, 31 comments
  24. Apple's 512GB Mac Studio vanishes, a quiet acknowledgment of the RAM shortage 225 upvotes, 131 comments
  25. CLI RSS/Atom feed reader inspired by Taskwarrior, synced using Git 24 upvotes, 8 comments
  26. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  27. A decade of Docker containers 329 upvotes, 236 comments
  28. Accessing inactive union members through char 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 53 upvotes, 36 comments
  30. How Big Diaper absorbs billions of extra dollars from American parents 80 upvotes, 115 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 9 March 2026. Source here.
